text:
The existing `receipts` table stores surface, transaction id, revision, changed summary, commands, artifact ref, and timestamp.  That is useful for `explain`, but not enough for a value meter.

You need a separate **`reconcile_commits`** or **`trellis_commits`** ledger that records every transaction, including:

* no-op commits;
* unchanged recomputes;
* suppressed publishes;
* empty output frames;
* command count by kind;
* duration;
* graph size;
* oracle result;
* trigger/fact kind;
* surface mode;
* whether host effects were applied.

Do not overload `receipts`. Receipts explain artifacts. Commit ledgers measure Trellis.
 [...] ### 4.1 The all-commit ledger (adopt the critique fully)

The single most important build item, and the critique is right that it must **not** overload `receipts` — receipts explain *artifacts* (the 30315, the injection), and only exist for effectful commits (`status_seam.rs:48`, `subscriptions.rs:54`). The value/no-op question needs **every** transaction. New table:

```sql
-- state/trellis_commits.rs (new)
CREATE TABLE trellis_commits (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  surface TEXT, txn_id INTEGER, revision INTEGER,
  mode TEXT,                         -- SurfaceMode at commit time
  trigger_kind TEXT, trigger_ref TEXT,   -- which InputFact / method drove it
  changed_inputs_json TEXT, changed_derived_json TEXT, changed_collections_json TEXT,  -- LABELS not ids (§4.2)
  resource_commands_json TEXT, output_frames_json TEXT,
  command_count INTEGER, output_count INTEGER, effect_count INTEGER, suppressed_count INTEGER,
  noop INTEGER,                      -- committed but changed nothing
  oracle_status TEXT, oracle_error TEXT,  -- sampled (§4.3)
  duration_us INTEGER, graph_nodes INTEGER, graph_resources INTEGER,
  created_at INTEGER
);
```

Written at each drive seam (add a `record_commit` sibling to `record_receipt` in `instrument.rs`). This makes `probe stats` answer the real questions the original doc's counters approximated: publishes suppressed (status commits where `command_count==0`), refresh-vs-replace split, subs open/close balance (`Σopen − Σclose == live_now`), hook renders that were `FrameKind::Unchanged` (already computed at `receipt.rs:56`, currently discarded), and the per-commit `duration_us`/`graph_nodes` histogram that answers "is the clone cost acceptable at real load" — measured where it happens. [...] 
Trellis's promise (README, `~/src/trellis/README.md:148-164`) is that the receipt "is the product": systems built on it are *legible* — diffable, replayable, explainable to tooling and agents "without parsing logs" — and *provably honest* via `assert_incremental_equals_full()`. The shipped tenex-edge adoption (`docs/trellis-adoption.md`) claims three concrete wins: the subscription leak is dead, five status-publish triggers collapsed to one deduped path, and the hook-context receipt cannot drift from the render. Every one of those claims is currently backed by **unit tests only**. The oracle runs in `#[cfg(test)]`. Receipts persist only for commits that produced effects (`status_seam.rs:48`, `daemon/server/subscriptions.rs:54`), so the most valuable events for the value question — the *suppressed* publishes, the *no-op* recomputes — leave no trace at all. Nobody can currently answer "did Trellis earn its keep this week" from a running fabric. [...] 2. **Value meter.** The current receipts ledger does not record the most important value evidence. Status receipts are skipped when no event is published.  Subscription receipts are skipped when no open/close effect occurs.  Therefore “how much work did Trellis suppress?” cannot be answered from persisted data today.
 [...] ### 3. Node IDs are currently insufficient evidence

`changed_summary_json` persists graph-local numeric `NodeId`s as arrays of integers. Its own comment says these are graph-local numeric ids.  A receipt saying `{"inputs":[2,3]}` is not a durable explanation. It is only useful while someone remembers how the graph was built.

Phase 0 should not merely “label receipt node ids.” It should introduce a **stable node label registry** per surface:

```text
status/<session>/working
status/<session>/title
status/<session>/activity
status/<session>/channels
status/<session>/ttl-arm
subscriptions/daemon/channels
subscriptions/daemon/addressed-pubkeys
subscriptions/session/<session>/channels
hook/<session>/cursor
hook/<session>/presence
hook/<session>/messages
```

Persist both id and label:

```json
{
  "inputs": [
    {"id": 12, "label": "status/s1/activity"}
  ],
  "derived": [
    {"id": 18, "label": "status/s1/content"}
  ],
  "collections": [
    {"id": 19, "label": "status/s1/coll"}
  ]
}
``` [...] ### 4.2 Stable node-label registry (adopt fully — Phase 0)

The shipped `changed_summary` persists graph-local integer ids (`{"inputs":[2,3]}`, `instrument.rs:112`) — useless to a human or agent a day later. Build a per-surface `NodeLabels{ by_id: BTreeMap<NodeId, String> }` populated at node creation (the reconcilers already name every node: `"activity"`, `"daemon-channels"`, `hook`'s six inputs are labeled at `hook_context/mod.rs:180-187`). Labels are stable, semantic paths: `status/<session>/activity`, `subscriptions/session/<session>/channels`, `hook/<session>/cursor`. Persist **both** id and label in the commits ledger and receipts v2. This is the precondition for every legibility claim — an agent cannot diagnose from `[2,3]`. [...] ### Step 2 — Node-label registry (`src/reconcile/labels.rs`, NEW)
```rust
pub struct NodeLabels { by_id: BTreeMap<NodeId, String> }   // Default + Clone
  fn new()
  fn record(&mut self, id: NodeId, label: impl Into<String>)   // called AT node creation
  fn label_of(&self, id: NodeId) -> Option<&str>
  fn labels_for(&self, ids: &[NodeId]) -> Vec<String>          // unregistered → "node:<n>"
  fn len(&self) / is_empty(&self) / iter() -> impl Iterator<(NodeId,&str)>
```
Re-exported as `crate::reconcile::NodeLabels`. Each reconciler now owns a `labels: NodeLabels` field, populated at node creation, exposed via **`pub fn labels(&self) -> &NodeLabels`** and **`pub fn graph_node_count(&self) -> usize`** (on all three: `StatusReconciler`, `SubscriptionReconciler`, `HookContextReconciler`). Semantic paths recorded:
- status: `status/<id>/{working,title,activity,channels,arm,content,coll}`
- subscriptions: `subscriptions/daemon/{channels,addressed_pubkeys,archived_channels,live_channels,subs}`, `subscriptions/session/<id>/{channels,subs}`
- hook: `hook/<session>/{cursor,now,channel-meta,members,presence,messages,view}` (the materialized output is keyed by `OutputKey`, not `NodeId`, so it is not in the id-registry — deliberate).
